name: Haley Stevens
subject: It's our campaign-iversary!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
One year ago today, I launched my campaign for the U.S. Senate.

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9oYWxleWZvcm1pLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.h_niuaD3rJRRhIwoxhqzx1U5obksHLeMYvoAxp01OHo?) for more
---
# [It's our campaign-iversary!](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=6380051&post_id=195063798&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTk1MDYzNzk4LCJpYXQiOjE3NzY5MDI1MzMsImV4cCI6MTc3OTQ5NDUzMywiaXNzIjoicHViLTYzODAwNTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZE7WguolZ_5Qnto2DQ8PBQORB-bIBQnb1OPtlgrkVbg)
### One year ago today, I launched my campaign for the U.S. Senate.
| | [Haley Stevens](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---
| Apr 23
---
| [](https://substack.com/@haleyformi)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvaGFsZXlmb3JtaS9wL2l0cy1vdXItY2FtcGFpZ24taXZlcnNhcnk_.-oMYocxPUQJGdefWKUkplV6R-dYDmPd5Lj5jrWKkfV8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/haleyformi/p/its-our-campaign-iversary?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Friends,
We've come a long way since launching this campaign one year ago.
Together, we have built more than just a campaign to keep Michigan blue. We've built a movement that is by the people, for the people, focused on all the things that make our state beautiful.
Grit. Hard work. Determination. Our core values of listening to your neighbor, learning from your community, and then leading with the same doggedness that built this state brick by brick.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2b3df7c0-74b0-47f3-bf50-b14aa9fdb6a6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[That's what I'm most proud of, friends.](https://substack.com/redirect/9427a5fe-92a2-43d5-b3d4-f45046978560?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
Even as I see the polling numbers -- which show that I'm in a statistical tie with Mike Rogers -- it never leaves my mind that all of this is because of folks like you.
The folks who have been with us since Day One. The folks who just came across this campaign and knew straight away that a Hope Agenda is exactly what our state needs. The folks who have believed in me enough to chip in to this campaign, to fill out surveys, to knock on doors, and to talk to friends and family members about this race.
**[We've got a long way to go yet.](https://substack.com/redirect/9427a5fe-92a2-43d5-b3d4-f45046978560?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** The GOP is dead set on taking this seat, and they're throwing everything they've got at us to try to buy this seat.
But since Day One, we've proven that this campaign has what it takes to win. Polls have consistently shown that I'm the candidate who can win for Democrats, keep this seat blue, and help flip the Senate.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3d96462b-5583-4642-9e37-d3998c5092f7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
From now until November, I'm going to be giving this campaign my all to make sure we keep up this momentum so we can continue building something Michiganders can be proud of.
And bring home the win on Nov. 3.
[Thank you for being in this with me, friends. You're the backbone of this campaign, and I couldn't be more grateful.](https://substack.com/redirect/9427a5fe-92a2-43d5-b3d4-f45046978560?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
With grit and gratitude,
Haley
* * *

A personal note from Haley 👋

I'm a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. I've spent my career fighting for Michigan's working families - and now I'm running for the U.S. Senate to keep standing up for the people I love.

But here's the truth: Senate control could come down to Michigan. And Trump and his megadonors know it. That's why they're gearing up to spend tens of millions to defeat me and hand this seat to a MAGA Republican.

I need your help to fight back. This race will be one of the most competitive - and expensive - in the country, and I'm counting on grassroots support to win.

If you're with me, will you chip in today?
[Donate](https://substack.com/redirect/9427a5fe-92a2-43d5-b3d4-f45046978560?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
###### Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate.

---
| | | [Restack](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9vcGVuLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9wdWIvaGFsZXlmb3JtaS9wL2l0cy1vdXItY2FtcGFpZ24taXZlcnNhcnk_.-oMYocxPUQJGdefWKUkplV6R-dYDmPd5Lj5jrWKkfV8?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---

(C) 2026 Haley for MI
33717 Woodward Ave #539, Birmingham, MI 48009
[Unsubscribe](https://substack..GYGAY07sG2m-kGhSmrSDCHNWtQQXGfcJJswwFnaL-7I?)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/6c0f2542-efaa-4d59-807d-429a56b6bfcb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[](https://substack..Z2X8LqxSLvrQiY69q0DNus2-rhJ8VSB0sekh7p_MLug?)

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: Canary in the coal mine
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Stats like this from California are the canary in the coal mine, Team. Kern County went from 332 uncounted, late ballots in 2024 to 3,303 in 2025. In Riverside County it was almost 6,000 uncounted, late ballots, more than double 2024's total.
Remember: These are from eligible voters, postmarked on time, sent ahead of or on Election Day. They should have counted -- except Trump's changes to the USPS made them arrive too late.
MAGA is trying every trick in the book to end the use of mail-in ballots or make them not count. RUSH $35 or whatever you can to help us reach voters in time to make every voice heard in the midterms and beyond >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=042226_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
LA Times: California election experts sound alarm as rate of rejected ballots quadruples [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=042226_3&amount=25]
Here's the deal, Team: Due to USPS changes , a huge number of mail-in ballots arrived too late to be counted in last year's special election in California.
A review by the Los Angeles Times showed that " ballots came in late at an average rate four times higher than that of the 2024 election ." By California law, vote-by-mail ballots are considered late if they do not arrive within seven days of election day, even if they are postmarked on or ahead of election day.
What does this mean? There is now concrete proof that Trump's changes to the U.S. Postal Service are aiding his attacks on mail-in voting . California voters did their job and mailed their ballot in accordance with their laws. Trump's attacks on the Postal Service aren't letting them deliver those ballots on time.
Team, every voter needs to know about this massive problem. Will you pitch in $35 or whatever you can today and help us spread the word about the USPS changes so we can protect mail-in ballots? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=042226_3&amount=35]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=042226_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=042226_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=042226_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=042226_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=042226_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=042226_3&amount=35]
Election officials warned before that 2025 special election that the Postal Service changes could delay the postmarking of ballots and lead to votes not being counted. Trump's USPS leadership shrugged after reducing the number of post office pickups in a quest for "efficiency".
“ Something changed ,” said Melvin E. Levey, who heads the Merced County Registrar of Voters. “ We don’t like seeing late ballots and if someone has made the effort to vote, we want to count it .” Semi-rural Merced County saw almost a sevenfold increase in late-arriving mail ballots in the November election compared with the year before.
We have to make sure every voter understands this change and how it can impact them -- from vote-by-mail, to bills, taxes, legal replies to companies, so much more. What Americans think is the mailing deadline may not be the deadline anymore and that's a huge deal that's generally being under-reported by the media.
Every vote should count, especially those postmarked and mailed ahead of or on election day. Pitch in $35 to help us make that a reality by informing voters about these USPS changes and holding USPS leadership accountable to Americans who deserve on this vital service >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=042226_3&amount=35]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042226_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=042226_3&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. name: Howard Dean
subject: 2 points. In Iowa!!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls]
I’ll get straight to it.
A new poll shows Zach Wahls trailing Republican Ashley Hinson by just 2 points.
In a statewide Senate race. In Iowa!
If Democrats are going to retake the majority, it will require winning races exactly like this one. That’s why I’m asking you to split a donation between Zach Wahls and Resist Reclaim Rebuild right now. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls]
DONATE NOW TO FLIP IOWA BLUE! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls]
Here’s the math: Democrats need a net gain to retake the Senate majority. There are only a handful of true pickup opportunities in the entire country this cycle, and this poll shows that Iowa is one of them.
Trump carried the state by double digits in 2024, but now, Wahls has narrowed that lead to 2. That’s why this race matters so much.
As someone who has spent a lot of time campaigning in Iowa, I know Zach has what it takes to win. Zach isn’t some placeholder candidate. He’s a 6th-generation Iowan who has spent his career standing up for working families and taking on powerful interests. He’s built real grassroots support without taking a cent from corporate PACs.
And now he’s within 2 points.
But here’s what I’ve learned over decades in politics: when a Democrat gets this close in a red-leaning state, national Republicans don’t sit back. They flood the zone with money and try to shut it down early.
If Zach has the resources to define this race, expand outreach, and reach persuadable voters before outside money drowns him out, he can win. If he doesn’t, Republicans will try to bury this opportunity before it fully takes shape.
Zach can win this race. But he cannot do it alone.
If you care about Democrats retaking the Senate, I’m asking you to split a contribution between Zach Wahls’ campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls]
CHIP IN TODAY TO HELP DEMS WIN! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-wahls]
Iowa is in play. Let’s not miss this opportunity.
Howard Dean
Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide.
PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW.RESISTRECLAIMREBUILD.COM) AND NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE COMMITTEE.
Resist Reclaim Rebuild
1331 FLORIDA AVE NW
Washington, DC 20009
United States

name: Sarah McBride
subject: This is why I only answer to Delaware
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
I wanted to reach out personally to share something with you today.
# [](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1269?t=1&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)
When I think about the work we’re doing in Congress, what inspires me the most is the people. Grassroots supporters like you who are committed to fighting for a Delaware where everyone has a chance to thrive.
That’s why I wanted to reach out personally to share something with you today.
**Right now, our average online donation is just $38.50.**
That number means something to me because it tells a story. Of a teacher in Wilmington pitching in what they can after a long day of educating our kids. A small business owner in Dover who believes we can grow our economy while protecting workers’ rights at the same time. A young community organizer in Newark who wants to see leaders in government with an agenda that’s rooted in dignity, fairness, and care.
**This is what our movement is made of.** We’re sustained by everyday people who believe in what we can do when courage and compassion drive our work.
I don’t answer to big money, I answer to my neighbors in Delaware. Every vote I cast and every bill I sponsor in Congress is grounded in the belief that government should serve everyone, not just the wealthy few at the top.
[**With all that said, will you chip in $38.50 or anything you can spare today to help power my campaign? Meeting our fundraising goals is critical to keep me fighting for Delaware families in Congress.**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1420?c0=mcb-em-20260422-2.ueV6BN&refcode=em20260422-3151&t=2&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)
**[Donate $38.50 »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1420?c0=mcb-em-20260422-2.ueV6BN&refcode=em20260422-3151&t=3&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)**
Thank you for being a part of this movement with me.
With gratitude,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-2026-footer?refcode=em20260422-2&t=4&refcode2=3151_21716_Q-Fvbd&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/receive_fewer_emails/?t=5&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/update_your_information/?t=6&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=3151%2E21716%2EQ-Fvbd)

Email us: [info@sarahmcbride.com](mailto:info@sarahmcbride.com)
Paid for by McBride for Delaware

subject: I’m not here to sugarcoat this, Friend.

body:
No fundraising fluff. Just a serious conversation about what happens next.

[](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
[]() Friend,
I’ll be blunt: this isn’t one of those emails full of flashy graphics, made-up deadlines, or “last chance” gimmicks.

You’ve seen enough of those.

This is different, because I respect you enough to tell you the truth. The fight for the Senate is real, and it’s coming down to just a few races. Mine could be the difference.

[**But I’m not blasting this to everyone. I’m only sharing this page with the supporters I consider my top circle. You’re on that list.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)

[SEE MY MESSAGE](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)

[**Take a look at what I laid out: no gimmicks, no sugarcoating, and no lies or fake urgency.**](https://link.email.mikerogersforsenate.)
Thanks for standing with me. You’re one of the few I can trust with this.
— Mike Rogers

body:

[View in Web Browser](https://link.email.freethinkerdaily.com/click/e128410/VaHR0cHM6Ly9vZGIubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3Yva2tuNG1BM2Q2bQ/SZW1haWxfZnJlZXRoaW5rZXJkYWlseV9jb20yNjA0MjM/LbmNsTWw2dVp2OA/qP2hpZHM9bmNrTll1ZGJwU3hvUnJscw/gaelmGg/sem3fcb40d5)

---
**DEMOCRATIC LEADERS ARE IN COMPLETE HYSTERIA OVER VOTER ID.**
A recent Pew poll shows that 71% of Democrats support Voter ID. Yet their leaders REFUSE to put their people first by passing the SAVE America Act in the Senate.
The SAVE America Act is straightforward: Require photo ID to vote and proof of citizenship to register. Long story short...

clean_body: We've got big elections this year, and we need you, Peter | = Hey Peter, I wanted to make sure you saw this: Today is your very last chance to activate your 2026 EMILYs List membership! = | = With the Trump administration actively working to roll back rights and freedoms that Democratic pro-choice women have fought so hard to protect, the stakes couldn't be higher. And EMILYs List members are a big reason we're still in this fight. Our members are at the heart of this community. Your support powers everything we do to find, train, and elect the Democratic pro-choice women who will stand up for all of us. This is the work — and you are a critical part of it. ******=****** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Jessica Mackler (she/her/hers) President, EMILYs List | = | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. clean_body: Peter, Mike Collins here, and I hate to tell you this... Chuck Schumer is flooding the airwaves AGAIN ... ATTACKING Voter ID. He's calling it "extreme." He's calling it " dangerous." He's calling it " JIM-CROW 2.0." Let me tell you what's actually dangerous. An election system that doesn't verify who is voting. HERE 'S WHAT'S HAPPENING The Radical Left has decided that requiring proof of citizenship is somehow "controversial." They want you to believe asking for ID is oppression. President Trump said it plainly: Elon Musk said it even more directly: That's not dramatic. That 's reality. WHY THIS MATTERS We are one bad election away from watching the Senate flip. One WEAK rule. One LOOSE standard. One close race decided by CHAOS. Our Senate majority is razor-thin. THIS IS WHERE YOU COME IN I'm not sending you a fundraising pitch right now. I'm sending you a question. They are watching the numbers. They are watching the response. And silence will be counted as opposition. I need to know where you stand: DO YOU SUPPORT A NATIONAL VOTER ID REQUIREMENT? • • Cast your vote now and make your voice heard. This is about something simple: Thank you, Mike Collins (R-GA) Candidate for United States Senate PAID FOR BY MIKE COLLINS FOR SENATE PO BOX 6491 ATHENS, GA 30604 Hey, if you're still reading this, don't forget to follow Mike Collins for Senate on and and TEXT MIKE TO 70622 If you would like to opt-out of important updates like this, please .

name: Elizabeth Guzman
subject: VA has new lines -- and I’m running for Congress!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Guzmán for Congress
clean_body: | | | Friend — Last night, Virginia approved new Congressional districts, in a STUNNING rebuke of MAGA fascism. Today, I'm launching my campaign to turn the new Virginia 7th District blue, and I'll be Donald Trump's worst nightmare. I’m mom, immigrant, union member, and state Delegate Elizabeth Guzman. I came to this country with $300 and worked three minimum wage jobs to build a life for my family. I became a social worker — and then the first immigrant Latina elected to the Virginia General Assembly. I've helped turn Virginia into a blue bulwark of resistance, successfully organizing for decades to pass laws ENDING local cooperation with ICE. I've passed state laws to stop abortion bans, protect democracy, provide paid sick leave, and expand collective bargaining rights. Thousands of donors like you powered our campaigns to defeat TWO anti-choice, pro-Trump Republican incumbents in the Virginia General Assembly. Now, we're running to turn a newly-drawn House district blue ... a MUST-WIN seat to FIRE Speaker Mike Johnson and END MAGA control of Washington. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. I’m not taking corporate money, PAC money, or billionaire money. This campaign is powered by people like you. And you’ll be who I fight for in Washington. In solidarity, Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Congress in Virginia's 7th District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. To donate by check, send to: Guzman for Congress PO Box 74, Nokesville VA, 20182 \- \- Paid for by Guzmán for Congress. Contributions or gifts to Friends of Elizabeth Guzman are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Elizabeth Guzman, please .

| Dear Peter, Did you know even 10 year olds are concerned about the price of gas? At a recent event I noticed a boy in the audience (can’t get them exposed to civic engagement too early - great job, Mom!). I asked him what his biggest concern was in the world today. His answer: the price of gas. I was surprised! He said that his mom and his sister complain everyday about how much it costs them to drive him back and forth to practice. This struck me because even children feel the economic crisis we are facing. When I think about how many of us can’t afford to live where we work, how many hours we spend commuting to work, and all of the stress of traffic, I worry about what this does to our spendable income, mental health, physical health and quality of life. A contract driver I met recently said he’s not even making minimum wage anymore because the price of gas has gone up so much. Transportation and production are immediately impacted which then limits spending overall, hurting our local economies. The high cost of fuel impacts more and more of our daily lives, way beyond the gas station. I realize times are tough for so many – it is critical that we unite and give of our time, talent and treasure sacrificially. **Please[make a contribution today](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984727/616250897/-148599213?utm_campaign=0422&refcode=20260422&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTk4NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2JjMjA2ZGEtYWYzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q8AM_uqWWewk-Ake14z6z3ymcHPfcy8yRntLBeMRq5E=). **Every dollar brings us one step closer to a government that truly represents and serves everyone. Thank you for your time, your faith in a just and humane future, and your support. Together we can get the government to prioritize the American people and lower the prices of gas! We’ve got this! -_Lisa_ Learn more by following me on [Facebook](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984728/616250898/2119237959?utm_campaign=0422&refcode=20260422&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTk4NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2JjMjA2ZGEtYWYzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q8AM_uqWWewk-Ake14z6z3ymcHPfcy8yRntLBeMRq5E=&emci=8c961df4-ae3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=cbc206da-af3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=18611292), [Instagram](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984729/616250899/1527795600?utm_campaign=0422&refcode=20260422&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTk4NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2JjMjA2ZGEtYWYzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q8AM_uqWWewk-Ake14z6z3ymcHPfcy8yRntLBeMRq5E=&emci=8c961df4-ae3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=cbc206da-af3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=18611292), [Bluesky](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984730/616250900/-1926114553?utm_campaign=0422&refcode=20260422&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTk4NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2JjMjA2ZGEtYWYzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q8AM_uqWWewk-Ake14z6z3ymcHPfcy8yRntLBeMRq5E=&emci=8c961df4-ae3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=cbc206da-af3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=18611292) and [TikTok](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984731/616250901/2069898425?utm_campaign=0422&refcode=20260422&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTk4NDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiY2JjMjA2ZGEtYWYzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Q8AM_uqWWewk-Ake14z6z3ymcHPfcy8yRntLBeMRq5E=&emci=8c961df4-ae3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=cbc206da-af3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=18611292).

clean_body: | = = | Dear Peter, Did you know even 10 year olds are concerned about the price of gas? At a recent event I noticed a boy in the audience (can’t get them exposed to civic engagement too early - great job, Mom!). I asked him what his biggest concern was in the world today. His answer: the price of gas. I was surprised! He said that his mom and his sister complain everyday about how much it costs them to drive him back and forth to practice. This struck me because even children feel the economic crisis we are facing. When I think about how many of us can’t afford to live where we work, how many hours we spend commuting to work, and all of the stress of traffic, I worry about what this does to our spendable income, mental health, physical health and quality of life. A contract driver I met recently said he’s not even making minimum wage anymore because the price of gas has gone up so much. Transportation and production are immediately impacted which then limits spending overall, hurting our local economies. The high cost of fuel impacts more and more of our daily lives, way beyond the gas station. I realize times are tough for so many – it is critical that we unite and give of our time, talent and treasure sacrificially. Please=. Every dollar brings us one step closer to a government that truly represents and serves everyone. Thank you for your time, your faith in a just and humane future, and your support. Together we can get the government to prioritize the American people and lower the prices of gas! We’ve got this! -Lisa Learn more by following me on , , and . | | = Paid for by Lisa D. Ramirez For Congress Lisa D. Ramirez For Congress PO Box 10060 Santa Ana, CA 92711 United States | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | | = | = If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please

name: Team Bell
subject: Clean air and water are non-negotiable.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
No one should have to second-guess the water coming out of their tap or worry about what their kids are breathing in. Peter,

Earth Day comes around once a year, but Wesley is fighting to protect our environment every single day.

No one should have to second-guess the water coming out of their tap or worry about what their kids are breathing in. And no community should be left dealing with the consequences while polluters walk away without being held accountable.

That's why Wesley has been pushing in Congress for stronger protections for clean air and clean water, real enforcement against polluters, and investments that actually reach the communities that have been ignored for too long. _And it's why his work is getting recognized._

**Wesley is proudly endorsed by the League of Conservation Voters** — a national organization that has spent decades holding polluters and anti-environment politicians accountable, electing climate champions, and fighting for policies that protect clean air, clean water, and public health across the country. **Their endorsement is a testament to their trust in this campaign, because he will always stand up to special interests and deliver real environmental progress.**

But getting things done in Congress takes resources, especially when we're up against deep-pocketed special interests who are willing to sacrifice our environment to further their agenda, no matter the cost. Wesley believes we have the power to take them on and build something better at the same time. Investing in clean energy means creating good-paying union jobs right here at home. It means growing our economy in a way that actually works for people — and for our planet.

[**This Earth Day, if you agree it's time to get serious about clean air, safe water, and a future that works for everyone, chip in anything you can to support Wesley's work. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984812/616254531/114933129?refcode=em26_260422_ethday_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260422_ethday_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=b84cf0c4-b03e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYjg0Y2YwYzQtYjAzZS1mMTExLThlZjItMDAwZDNhMTRiNjQwIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=zq71Qu4hE18Tl-mYWqiBRGfHYpVVmpmTB6Hlp97eR_s=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [$10 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129984813/616254532/114933129?refcode=em26_260422_ethday_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260422_ethday_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=b84cf0c4-b03e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640

clean_body: No one should have to second-guess the water coming out of their tap or worry about what their kids are breathing in. Peter, Earth Day comes around once a year, but Wesley is fighting to protect our environment every single day. No one should have to second-guess the water coming out of their tap or worry about what their kids are breathing in. And no community should be left dealing with the consequences while polluters walk away without being held accountable. That's why Wesley has been pushing in Congress for stronger protections for clean air and clean water, real enforcement against polluters, and investments that actually reach the communities that have been ignored for too long. And it's why his work is getting recognized. Wesley is proudly endorsed by the League of Conservation Voters — a national organization that has spent decades holding polluters and anti-environment politicians accountable, electing climate champions, and fighting for policies that protect clean air, clean water, and public health across the country. Their endorsement is a testament to their trust in this campaign, because he will always stand up to special interests and deliver real environmental progress. But getting things done in Congress takes resources, especially when we're up against deep-pocketed special interests who are willing to sacrifice our environment to further their agenda, no matter the cost. Wesley believes we have the power to take them on and build something better at the same time. Investing in clean energy means creating good-paying union jobs right here at home. subject: Earth is the only home we've got.

Happy Earth Day, Peter!

Earth is our home, and here in WA-08, we're lucky to live in one of the most beautiful corners of it.

From Mount Rainier to the Enchantments, and everything in between, breathtaking beauty surrounds us here. Washington State is where we live our lives, where our kids will grow up, and the place we all call home.

I've spent my time in Congress trying to protect that home, Peter. That means defending our public lands, fighting for clean air and water, and fighting back when extremists try to roll those protections back, including our president, Donald Trump, and his Republican yes-men.

They've made it easier for polluters to get away with harming communities. And too often, they've dismissed or outright denied the science on climate change—even as we're seeing more wildfires, flooding, and extreme weather at home and across the country. These are all issues we feel right here in WA-08. We see it in our forests, our snowpack, and the seasons changing in ways that affect daily life. Protecting this place shouldn't be controversial. It's about making sure our kids inherit something just as good— _or better_ —than what we have now.

[**If you agree it's time to stop denying climate change and start actually protecting our environment, add your name to my petition today. Help me demand that Congressional Republicans take climate change seriously. →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129985093/616261384/2056092354?ms=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&refcode=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&source=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=3VuJ1+98pzz0xvFxvhyQp33y7G/kh+x3x8/IxjOf1K6uCOr6XvBw06DuRtT4CyK43Z21QthsnF0RE4H62+TJx9z8HtW0E9WSH0CdJAGXUCotfIhcWNiO6hGeR81LQAwrIa42mKstPofgi4dhQJ6/WK83BTdSoJvb4as2q87x1oo=&refcode2=c945c1fb-b33e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTQ1YzFmYi1iMzNlLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A3Hxllzdf4Bj8Vf6TtefPHwezsukb_jMMVhghAbWHOk=&emci=a7bff739-5e3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=c945c1fb-b33e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=15820338) [SIGN THE PETITION](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/129985094/616261385/2056092354?ms=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&refcode=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&source=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_campaign=em26_260422_ethday_adv_gq_act___KS_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=3VuJ1+98pzz0xvFxvhyQp33y7G/kh+x3x8/IxjOf1K6uCOr6XvBw06DuRtT4CyK43Z21QthsnF0RE4H62+TJx9z8HtW0E9WSH0CdJAGXUCotfIhcWNiO6hGeR81LQAwrIa42mKstPofgi4dhQJ6/WK83BTdSoJvb4as2q87x1oo=&refcode2=c945c1fb-b33e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOTQ1YzFmYi1iMzNlLWYxMTEtOGVmMi0wMDBkM2ExNGI2NDAiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=A3Hxllzdf4Bj8Vf6TtefPHwezsukb_jMMVhghAbWHOk=&emci=a7bff739-5e3e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&emdi=c945c1fb-b33e-f111-8ef2-000d3a14b640&ceid=15820338) Thank you,

| | | | High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.

Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.

At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts.

By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch. She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground. Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina.

Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks.

She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.

Nancy is fighting to:

* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast

* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency

* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free

* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate

* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think

* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front.

South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Asking personally
body:
Don't look away, team.

+------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Sherrod Brown (D) | Jon Husted (R) |
|-------------------------------------+----------------------------------|
| [ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260422_2.flRqgH&refcode=em20260422-2&t=1001&akid=6265%2E582294%2Evn33OX ]Sherrod Brown | [ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260422_2.flRqgH&refcode=em20260422-2&t=1002&akid=6265%2E582294%2Evn33OX ]Jon Husted |
|-------------------------------------+----------------------------------|
| 47% | 50% |
+------------------------------------------------------------------------+

First, polling showed me trailing my corrupt opponent, Jon Husted, by 1
point.

Then, National Republicans announced they're pouring $79 million into Ohio
to stop our campaign – the most they're spending in any battleground
state.

And now, a brand new poll has me slipping behind, this time trailing
Husted by 3 points.

Their attacks are working. And they’re just getting started.

Millions of dollars of ads targeting me are hitting the airwaves soon. The
only way we can win this toss up race and flip Ohio and the Senate is with
a massive outpouring of grassroots support today, April 22.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260422_2.flRqgH&refcode=em20260422-2&t=1003&akid=6265%2E582294%2Evn33OX ]We haven’t hit our $200,000 emergency goal yet, so I'm asking you
personally: Will you rush $5 – split between my campaign and
Chris Pappas – to help us respond to the GOP's attacks before it's too
late?

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20260422_2.flRqgH&amount=5&refcode=em20260422-2&t=1022&akid=6265%2E582294%2Evn33OX ]Donate $5 »

Thank you.

Sherrod

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.chrispappas.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=6265%2E582294%2Evn33OX

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Right now President Trump has one on the US Secret Service. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | Right now President Trump has one on the US Secret Service. Its name is Spot. | And you can find it patrolling outside of Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate with the “United States Secret Service - Technical Security Division” branded on its side. name: Katie Porter
subject: I just stepped off the debate stage
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)
[](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260422_2_ak.p3t4i9&refcode=em20260423-41196&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)
Peter, I just stepped off the stage from the first debate of this race for governor, and I want to tell you exactly what I saw up there.
**There were two very different visions for California.**
I’m fighting for working families—people stretched thin by skyrocketing costs, students buried in debt, parents struggling to afford child care.
The other candidates? _They’re fighting for themselves._
Steyer is a self-funding billionaire trying to buy his way into this race. Becerra took tens of thousands from Big Oil. And the two Republicans in this race are trying to bring Trump’s MAGA agenda to California.
_I’ve spent my career calling out exactly this kind of behavior._ When I bring out a whiteboard, it’s not for show—it’s because facts matter. People can see exactly who’s looking out for them and who isn’t.
As governor, I’ll do the same thing. I’m fighting for tuition-free public college, single-payer health care, eliminating state income tax for those earning under $100,000, and universal child care so parents don’t have to choose between a paycheck and their kids.
**The bottom line is I don’t take corporate PAC money, I’m not self-funding my campaign, and I’m not here to protect the status quo. I’m here to change it.**
California’s primary is coming up fast, and only the top two candidates move on to November. If we’re going to win this, we need to make sure we’re one of them.
**[If you’re with me, I’m asking you to chip in whatever you can today to help us reach more voters, and make sure we earn a spot on November’s ballot.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260422_2_ak.p3t4i9&refcode=em20260423-41196&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260422_2_ak.p3t4i9&refcode=em20260423-41196&t=23&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)
Thank you,
Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260423-41196&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=41196_3422442_IfrgvL&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)**
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=41196&t=25&akid=41196%2E3422442%2EIfrgvL)

Katie Porter for Governor 2026

body:
[Barlow Logo](https://barlowforwyo.com/)

Welcome to the Barlow for Wyoming Newsletter. Your hub for latest news, inf=
ormation and upcoming events.

=E2=80=8A

STEADY WORK, STRONG SUPPORT

Hi friends,
This past week took us across central and southern Wyoming, and like always=
, it=E2=80=99s the people who make the miles worth it.
=C2=A0
Kelly and I continue to be humbled by the conversations, encouragement, and=
clarity we hear everywhere we go. Whether it=E2=80=99s at an event, a quic=
k stop, or a longer visit, folks are sharing what matters to them, and we=
=E2=80=99re listening.
=C2=A0
I also had the opportunity to put a finer point on where I stand when it co=
mes to responsible energy development. I support Wyoming energy and the men=
and women who power this state. And I insist that private property and wat=
er rights are respected. That has been my record, and it will continue to b=
e. Wyoming can lead in energy and still do things the right way, on Wyoming=
=E2=80=99s terms. See my statement below.
=C2=A0
We=E2=80=99re especially excited and grateful for the endorsement and thoug=
htful words shared by Ann Simpson and Diana Enzi this week. Their families =
have given so much to Wyoming, and their support carries real meaning for u=
s. We don=E2=80=99t take that lightly.
=C2=A0
We also appreciated the shoutout from Protect Wyoming and the continued enc=
ouragement from so many across the state.
=C2=A0
More than anything, this week was a reminder of why we=E2=80=99re doing thi=
s. Wyoming is full of good people who care about their communities and want=
to see our state move forward without losing what makes it special.
=C2=A0
We=E2=80=99re thankful to be in it with you.

=C2=A0
=E2=80=94 Eric

[SUPPORT ERIC!](https://secure.winred.com/eric-barlow-32097f9d/donate?sc=3D=
winred-directory&money_bomb=3Dfalse&recurring=3Dfalse)

ow-represents-what-wyoming-conservatism-was-meant-to-be/article_fea398a3-0a=
52-4290-97e5-148a42a1b79e.html)

We have watched Wyoming politics for a long time. Long enough to know the d=
ifference between someone who wants to serve this state and someone who wan=
ts to use it. Wyoming does not need politics imported from somewhere else d=
ressed up in Wyoming clothes. And it does not need a politics so eager to t=
ear things down that it forgets how much care and responsibility it took to=
build what generations before us left behind. It needs leadership that gre=
w here and has been tested by solving one hard problem after the next.
That is why we support Eric Barlow.

Riding for Wyoming:
Responsible Energy Development
=C2=A0
Wyoming=E2=80=99s energy industry drives our jobs, communities, and future.

Energy Innovation Center, 1000 E Lewis St, Laramie, WY=C2=A0

On The Trail

GLENROCK
Our Glenrock Meet & Greet at the Higgins Hotel had a great turnout!
Thank you Laurie and Luis, and Rhonda Dilts for hosting the event.

DOUGLAS
While we were in Douglas, Kelly and I had the opportunity to check out East=
ern Wyoming College Douglas Campus. Really cool to see the Nursing program,=
the Gunsmithing program, and the progress on their new Construction buildi=
ng. Lots of good things happening out there.

GLENDO/WHEATLAND

We had a great stops in Glendo with a social studies class full of thoughtf=
ul questions (and fresh off a successful FFA state convention), then we wer=
e off to Guernsey for stops at the VFW and Senior Center, where we had a st=
rong conversation about property tax concerns.

We made it to Wheatland, where I connected with folks at the courthouse whi=
le Kelly met up with a great group and had a well-attended meet and greet i=
n Lusk. It is always appreciate hearing directly from folks across our comm=
unities.

Kelly and I had a great stop at Eastern Wyoming College and had a great tou=
r of Ag Technology and the new trailers they will be sending out to communi=
ties around Wyoming to help teach.

We had a good Meet and Greet at The Old Moose Coffee shop was well attended=
and great questions were asked!

Cheyenne
We were able to stop by for a visit with the leadership, staff and board of=
the Wyoming Association of Municipalities (WAM) in Cheyenne.

We had a great=C2=A0discussion on economic development in the region and th=
e work WAM is doing for collaboration across the state.

A great time with Cheyenne folks this week! The meet & greet at The Hawthor=
n Tree showed a great turnout, and Patron Night at the Old West Museum did =
not disappoint. We ended the night with a local meetup and lots of good con=
versation, hosted by friends Tony and Sandy Ross, with help from Diane Shob=
er.
Thanks to everyone who made our visit great.

Thank you to Denise and Roz as well as Larry Barttelbort who helped with hi=
s wife Susie for gathering friends for a meeting while we were in town.

Get In The Saddle

Wyoming=E2=80=99s strength has always come from neighbors pitching in. Join=
the Barlow For Wyoming team of volunteers who are helping share his messag=
e across our state.

=F0=9F=A4=A0 Ride with us =E2=80=94 sign up to volunteer!

[VOLUNTEER](https://www.barlowforwyo.com/contact)

Follow Us Online

Your hub for latest news, inf= ormation and upcoming events. =E2=80=8A STEADY WORK, STRONG SUPPORT Hi friends, This past week took us across central and southern Wyoming, and like always= , it=E2=80=99s the people who make the miles worth it. =C2=A0 Kelly and I continue to be humbled by the conversations, encouragement, and= clarity we hear everywhere we go. Whether it=E2=80=99s at an event, a quic= k stop, or a longer visit, folks are sharing what matters to them, and we= =E2=80=99re listening. =C2=A0 I also had the opportunity to put a finer point on where I stand when it co= mes to responsible energy development. I support Wyoming energy and the men= and women who power this state. And I insist that private property and wat= er rights are respected. That has been my record, and it will continue to b= e. Wyoming can lead in energy and still do things the right way, on Wyoming= =E2=80=99s terms. See my statement below. =C2=A0 We=E2=80=99re especially excited and grateful for the endorsement and thoug= htful words shared by Ann Simpson and Diana Enzi this week. Their families = have given so much to Wyoming, and their support carries real meaning for u= s. We don=E2=80=99t take that lightly. =C2=A0 We also appreciated the shoutout from Protect Wyoming and the continued enc= ouragement from so many across the state. =C2=A0 More than anything, this week was a reminder of why we=E2=80=99re doing thi= s. Wyoming is full of good people who care about their communities and want= to see our state move forward without losing what makes it special. =C2=A0 We=E2=80=99re thankful to be in it with you. =C2=A0 =E2=80=94 Eric = winred-directory&moneybomb=3Dfalse&recurring=3Dfalse IN THE NEWS Douglas Budget (print only)=C2=A0 Barlow campaigns in county at fundrasier and at Q&A = ow-represents-what-wyoming-conservatism-was-meant-to-be/articlefea398a3-0a= 52-4290-97e5-148a42a1b79e.html =C2=A0 We have watched Wyoming politics for a long time. name: Senator Tom Willis
subject: My Opponent Has Lost Her Way
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/238e790a5bc6/my-opponent-has-lost-her-way?e=a0285cc040)
---
|
---
| | | | | |

# My Opponent Has Lost Her Way

---
|
---
|
---
| |
Dear Friend,
West Virginians believe in something simple:
* **Faith.**

* **Family.**

* **Freedom.**

But somewhere along the way, **my opponent lost her way** — and it’s not hard to see why.
This isn’t just about votes in Washington.
It’s about **values** — and whether those values still reflect West Virginia.
Take the Second Amendment:
While **she supported red flag gun confiscation funding — putting your rights at risk without due process** **—** **I’ve earned an "A" rating from the NRA — the highest of any U.S. Senate candidate in West Virginia.**
**That’s a big difference.**
And it’s not the only one.
Time and again, she’s taken positions that don’t line up with the people of this state:
* She voted against **traditional marriage — one man and one woman.**

* She’s **flip-flopped on abortion** and backed **taxpayer funding for Planned Parenthood.**

* She failed to stand up for **protecting girls’ sports.**

* She didn’t push for the **SAVE Act — ensuring only American citizens vote in American elections.**

**That’s not where West Virginia stands.**
And it goes beyond policy.
**While you work hard to get ahead, she’s looked out for herself and her circle:**
* **A top insider trader in the U.S. Senate**

* **Helped secure her son’s appointment as U.S. Attorney — then voted for him**

**That’s not putting West Virginia first.**
And some of these decisions have real consequences:
* **Unvetted refugee resettlement.**

* **Attempts to expand government control over online speech.**

**These aren’t isolated votes — they’re a pattern.**
And people here see it.
Because West Virginians still believe:
* **One nation under God.**

* **Strong families.**

* **Parents — not government — raising their children.**

But too often, politicians go to Washington and forget who they represent.
They follow pressure.
They follow politics.
They follow the crowd.
**I won’t.**
I’m a Christian, a husband, and a father raising my family right here in West Virginia.
**I’ve served this state for decades — as a U.S. Army Special Forces Green Beret, in business, and in the State Senate.**
**As a Green Beret, I learned how to make tough decisions under pressure, stand firm in the face of opposition, and see the mission through.**
**Our values don’t change when you get elected.****
They matter even more.**
I’m running to put **West Virginia first again** — not Washington agendas, not special interests, not political convenience.
And you can count on me to fight for:
* **Securing our border**

* **Investing in West Virginia infrastructure**

* **Lowering costs by cutting government overreach**

**This race comes down to a simple choice:**
**Do you want more of the same from a career politician — or a proven conservative with real experience who will stand and fight for West Virginia?**
**If you believe it’s time for a Senator who stands firm — not one who’s lost her way —**
**Vote Tom Willis on May 12.**
**IT’S TIME FOR A CHANGE, WEST VIRGINIA.**
**Tom Willis**

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1985, the Coca-Cola Co. debuted New Coke, changing its signature formula after 99 years, but the new, sweeter drink sparked fierce backlash and became a textbook marketing blunder. [See the debut commercial here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0EFOtDAUAODTlN2Q0hYoiy7-PwYTndFEY9QV6fBeZ54WOmkLEzy90St4gW_xvR6GgyVP8-nBTmiyC8OM1-QxZ4zDGFIOLlpKNJ_sJ8HiwkqAYRBcNDuudkIOYMlvgyrAOESBskBTtW3TSa2FLM6mHlXdSmgAnK6a-uiqrkJdS9XakY8NFmR-NK6ErDhvOS-5BWetdprrTtljzRRHoBxi-RFozmd0fgsrxnIMU-HNOedLYvIfEz0T_RaWvJRHZKJ_foPpcX_7_6t7YbJPxOTNvEX5TmDT_R09XfZrEQ1cruQ9paZlip8mS_7XzX_KWI34DgAA__-E03ME). The [NFL Draft kicks off](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQgOGnwVssOIjtDAxdvKVrR-sc7pJrCURAbOXtq_QVOv_SN_xf5-WMEiVdP_FOvnFeEu01UmtUlkuuLXNBqZKu-CPhyXmTQHkBDcNBuwPYJaDE1-K64JkIyHbkzTgOJztNYLubN8CWjtbS6uxoLmxoALNOHIw9nlYcO_FvTTuwRutR615jYMSJJz2dHK5H5TQFabn031lSuxHHV96o9Jd876K_tfaoyn4omBXM-773VB_pHRXMiaOCORTkpmAu-ZlC7YoPj11ilDqMyunrHSX-Ye1fBzYPvwEAAP__6_JvzQ) tonight from Pittsburgh, its first time hosting since 1948, and we have all the details below. The cost of living varies wildly across the United States, and for retirees, choosing the wrong state can cost up to six figures a year. On today's episode of _The Flyover Podcast_ , host Ayla Brown shares new data that ranks the cost of living in every U.S. state, what groceries, housing, and healthcare actually cost depending on where you live. Plus, which states give retirees the most financial breathing room? Where does your state land, and what does it mean for your retirement? [Tune in here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFu6zAMgOHT2FsMmlJkedDwlmx5a0eBDqmErWIFsprUty_aK_QA_w98b-d4Js26Xv_TXUJLJa7y2rK0JjVeytZKqqSbrlf6UP5M5aksJSKgO4A9oIlMmvdoew5JBMX0EsZpcrPxHk1_C87M7C7Lwt4lHHF0s0VrSIR5ToDHXsPPDSyaEWACGIA4Efnkwc-WlmNnQVhbqcN70bXdJOW9PKUOl3Lvc7i19tg686_DU4enKkullYe8d3h6FJavVmnsa-DHS3PWzU2dheudNP_27U_oZ8DvAAAA__-JGGuG)! Mother's Day is closer than you think. ## Iran Seizes Ships as Ceasefire Teeters
Iran's Revolutionary Guard [fired on at least two vessels](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u6zAQhE9DdRTopX6ogsVr1Pm1KYW1uLQ2pkSDosU4pw-UK6QcDDCD7_u4TlfkwNv9P65ks4_TRmUPlDOlaY57jj4h77zd8cHu5ePBjuIECjqpGgl6csjhPTWVs54ISFdkL33fDdoY0NVilQcA9Ma3HerWmbaH2aAejMfW6G6o2J5rqgF9UapXqlboPKLxRpmhwVsrGkWOc0z1Z-QtL-TDOx6U6jmuVbBLzs9d6H8CRgFjKaX28eukOHsBY-CD5JkFjJxwk69dFkzyFuL8QEdyiWl9fUt8Jg4SoErWPQuHwHvXi0bdV-Tw-5X_JOiw8BMAAP__N-l7LQ) in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday and took custody of both ships, the first such seizures since the war began. Iran's parliament speaker called the U.S. blockade a "flagrant breach" of the ceasefire. President Trump, who extended the ceasefire Tuesday after a planned second round of peace talks in Pakistan fell apart, said Wednesday there is "no time pressure" on a deal and that the blockade "scares them more than bombing." Trump also announced that Iran [agreed to spare eight women protesters](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGS4yAQRL8GMrnwgBEKCC5R5ksvVM2JwZo7BC7A9vrvt7S1f7Bxv-qufn-uyxU5cb79xp18j2XJ9GqJeqe6rKX1Eity43zD_xwesTw5UFlAgR2UGUAvATm9FyODj0RAWpI_j6OdtHOg5eanyWhrVzNNlmwYiS4KnbYQ4-osQJTsjzZlQJ-VGpU6KQwR0UWn3GTw70UYRYF7qad_hXPfKKZ3eVI9rWWXyW-935vQvwTMAua-0cYpHZmAeSs7HYcEzBh2ztx6xc4lC5gvzmjtzMAVM2MeXgc7tDtWCgN90Pr4BmX14f7ilLjZURh125G_BmT_kbGnh88AAAD__0r6gLc) who had been scheduled for execution, with four released immediately and four sentenced to one month in prison. Senate Republicans blocked a [fifth attempt from Democrats](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ_GZKCmzfATOLiEbC69EDW4PdO3BiPbM9a8_Yp9hc1KKqlK3_fvvtxJvByPv7SzyS4sB5fkOWeOyxZSDi6SJDke9CX25cJbLIcFAfsauhr1Ykn8Z-kqaxwzsq7YtMPQT3ocUVdPY2_9aoHJtXparaUeB8YbwoSM7TZulZhrDTrULcAA0ABZRzS6Ecapo_WmOmArOcTmf5AjP9n5T3hzbLawV948cz6T0n8UzgrnUkqzremiuHqF8xUVzokPylzn-NrPulCsz1A4ploiHQrnKhp7FvFeUj-oDh47if95yL_S8jb4HQAA__8UHng6) to invoke the War Powers Resolution on the 54th day of the conflict, with the fast-approaching 60-day deadline set to force Congress to formally weigh in on continuing military operations.
## NFL Draft Kicks Off Tonight
The first round of the 2026 NFL Draft kicks off tonight from Pittsburgh, with the Las Vegas Raiders on the clock at No. 1 overall. It's the Steel City's first time hosting the event since 1948. [See the draft order](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQgOGnwVssOIjtDAxdvKVrR-sc7pJrCURAbOXtq_QVOv_SN_xf5-WMEiVdP_FOvnFeEu01UmtUlkuuLXNBqZKu-CPhyXmTQHkBDcNBuwPYJaDE1-K64JkIyHbkzTgOJztNYLubN8CWjtbS6uxoLmxoALNOHIw9nlYcO_FvTTuwRutR615jYMSJJz2dHK5H5TQFabn031lSuxHHV96o9Jd876K_tfaoyn4omBXM-773VB_pHRXMiaOCORTkpmAu-ZlC7YoPj11ilDqMyunrHSX-Ye1fBzYPvwEAAP__6_JvzQ). Indiana quarterback Fernando Mendoza is the odds-on favorite to be the Raiders' selection, meaning the draft _really_ starts at pick two. Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love is this year's X-factor--if the [Cardinals take him at No. 3](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb8Gb47wg9h4YOjiLV07Ws_mEb8UQwTUUfr1Vbr0A7pd6V7dI52Py3xBDhyv77iTrT7NkR4lUK2U5zWVmnxGLhyv-Mnuy6eDHaUZJPSt1C2o2SGH56wbZz0RkGrIdsPQj8oYUM1mERajvexw6bTrz7SMi9NeKj_imdZ-adi-3qQG1Uk5SHmS6Dyi8UaaUeNyFlqS45ry6ZY41o18eKaD8mlNexPsVuu9CPUmYBIwYebvFLHcU67ltRAwRR_-mnbF7DhiKAKmG2Xa-YlbG9JBLQiYVN8ZNb5Sk627PzgELv0gtLzuyOEXWv9l6rDwEwAA__8Dh32w), he'd be the highest running back drafted since Saquon Barkley in 2018. The 2026 class is widely considered weaker than usual because there's only _one_ franchise quarterback (Mendoza) and fewer elite prospects at the top. Chiefs general manager Brett Veach [predicted](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOmzAURp_GbEbGmOAMHtJWtEMTdajaEd3Y13BbgyNzS8TbV8kr_PunT-ec39fxCpRonW6woOOYxxWfW0JmLKPPG-dYgDZaJ_hL4V_MOwXMo1b6JJWRuh0DUDpGUwUXETW2Fbqm70_n1lrdVrNDE5VvlIUGNETofNc08e6V76Ppz1ZX5F5vyui2UapXqlYQIoCNVtmzgXsnjMJAnEv9J9PKM8Z05B1L7fNSJTczPzbRXoQehB5iznyHlOqCkKbltRF6eFJBhofQQ2eM7YUePs-EcZNfr_JTQWb5C8HP8kfBQJ43-Q1hP-TPAgHlxTPtxIe8sHyL34bv8kuByFVx4fGklGg79cKoaQFKbyz-UMvd6f8BAAD__2ybiHQ) "a lot of trades" as teams jockey for prospects with nearly identical grades. Round 1 wraps up tonight before Rounds 2-3 on Friday and Rounds 4-7 on Saturday. Coverage airs across ESPN, ABC, and the NFL Network, with the full broadcast starting tonight at 8 p.m. ET. [Here's how to watch it](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1ygzAQBOCngU7MWfyJQkUaOqdNyRzoZC4ROg9czPjtM_YrpN6Zb3b36zpdkRPn2ydu5DXKlOk8EqnSPi1yqMQd-eB8wx8Ov1EeHEgmC7Yz0BhbTwE5PaemDD4SWapL8pe-74baOVuXq3fLfAl120E7DEM_9711DhoICLadobUl-5cGja0vAD1ABRgioosO3NDg3BYNUGCVvfoWzrpSTE950F4tspXJr6r3o6g_CjsWdjzPs8oxvbLCjq8xhR3fdXNMJuwY1QRUOozyRodJsqCyZLPKaVTMibqsBnMwm-xU7j7cT06Jj64vGrhtyG-71H-d9fD2LwAA___2VX46).
## Cost of Raising a Child Per State
The national average cost of raising a child from birth to age 18 [topped $300,000 for the first time](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFyszAQhJ9GdDDnQwZRqPgbdf7blMwZncwlAnmQbIa3z5DJG6S6K3Z299uP23gjibI-_tPCtoQ0rrznyKXwNk4plxQ2kizrg77Ev0J6i-c0ImBXg66xHT1JPEZdeRuYkduK7aXvu6E1BttqtpO-kr-jvgzBIAD0xICM2BkkbNFUYk830NheAHqABsgHIhMMmEHT_ao0sJeStuYzyVpmDvFIb96aKS1VtHMpz6zafwqdQrfve_PKVJKn4xQodLmk7VDolrTyec84hQ70-aNCd3LWv5j1NEv09f2oc6HCCp0ZejCgrwC9Qldt1j93iVFy1ysNj4Uk_jQpf5rvbfE7AAD__4TKgJw), reaching $303,418 according to a new analysis that tracked housing, food, daycare, transportation, and health insurance. Hawaii is the most expensive state at $412,661, [nearly $50,000 more](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFugzAQhp_GbCBzdoEMHrqwpWtHdPGd4VrHjrATlLevUvUNuv_6P33f53k5o0RJ6wde2dWQl8RHiVwr74vPpeawoxRJK34L3UN-CHFeQMPQatuCWQglPhfbkAvMwKZh14_jcDLTBKbZnB4tB77YnsyIHs0JR-j9EKC35HUwjbjXm7Zgeq1HrTuNFBCnMOnpZPHypqxmkpr37itLqhuH-MwP3jufr010W623osy7glnBfBxHFzmRpLXuzK-Ngpn4UlufU8lRCKvkpGD-E2ux9ZtEaku901PB3OyObofEKGUYldXrFSX-wuq_Cj0c_AQAAP__dRF8aA) than second-place Alaska at $365,047. New Hampshire came in cheapest at just under $202,000. The total doesn't include college, which could add another $152,000. clean_body: The average cost of raising a child from birth to age 18 , reaching $303,418 according to a new analysis that tracked housing, food, daycare, transportation, and health insurance. | | | | | | Thursday, April 23, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in 1985, the Coca-Cola Co. debuted New Coke, changing its signature formula after 99 years, but the new, sweeter drink sparked fierce backlash and became a textbook marketing blunder. . The tonight from Pittsburgh, its first time hosting since 1948, and we have all the details below. The cost of living varies wildly across the United States, and for retirees, choosing the wrong state can cost up to six figures a year. On today's episode of The Flyover Podcast , host Ayla Brown shares new data that ranks the cost of living in every U.S. state, what groceries, housing, and healthcare actually cost depending on where you live. Plus, which states give retirees the most financial breathing room? Where does your state land, and what does it mean for your retirement? ! Mother's Day is closer than you think. Our has something for every kind of mom, from thoughtful to practical to a little over-the-top. Don't sleep on it. Today’s sponsor, , the fastest-growing software company of 2023, is turning everyday smartphones into passive income generators, with a pre-IPO opportunity now available for early investors. | Iran Seizes Ships as Ceasefire Teeters Iran's Revolutionary Guard in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday and took custody of both ships, the first such seizures since the war began. Iran's parliament speaker called the U.S. blockade a "flagrant breach" of the ceasefire. President Trump, who extended the ceasefire Tuesday after a planned second round of peace talks in Pakistan fell apart, said Wednesday there is "no time pressure" on a deal and that the blockade "scares them more than bombing." Trump also announced that Iran who had been scheduled for execution, with four released immediately and four sentenced to one month in prison. Senate Republicans blocked a to invoke the War Powers Resolution on the 54th day of the conflict, with the fast-approaching 60-day deadline set to force Congress to formally weigh in on continuing military operations. NFL Draft Kicks Off Tonight The first round of the 2026 NFL Draft kicks off tonight from Pittsburgh, with the Las Vegas Raiders on the clock at No. 1 overall. It's the Steel City's first time hosting the event since 1948. . Indiana quarterback Fernando Mendoza is the odds-on favorite to be the Raiders' selection, meaning the draft really starts at pick two. Notre Dame running back Jeremiyah Love is this year's X-factor--if the , he'd be the highest running back drafted since Saquon Barkley in 2018. The 2026 class is widely considered weaker than usual because there's only one franchise quarterback (Mendoza) and fewer elite prospects at the top. Chiefs general manager Brett Veach "a lot of trades" as teams jockey for prospects with nearly identical grades. Round 1 wraps up tonight before Rounds 2-3 on Friday and Rounds 4-7 on Saturday. Coverage airs across ESPN, ABC, and the NFL Network, with the full broadcast starting tonight at 8 p.m. ET. . Cost of Raising a Child Per State The national average cost of raising a child from birth to age 18 , reaching $303,418 according to a new analysis that tracked housing, food, daycare, transportation, and health insurance. Hawaii is the most expensive state at $412,661, than second-place Alaska at $365,047. New Hampshire came in cheapest at just under $202,000. Daycare remains the biggest driver, averaging $17,264 a year for infants, though that figure from a year ago. The total doesn't include college, which could add another $152,000. Overall, the 18-year price tag has risen nearly 28% since 2023, and a recent poll found 77% of Americans believe raising a family is harder now than for previous generations. See the full .
