**Good Morning!** On this day in 1923, future Democrat governor [Dolph Briscoe Jr.](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGSrCAQgOHTYKYFrTNqQPASs3nphhbSjfQuggXUOHP7rd0r7AX-v-r7eKwPw4Hj_t8cpOvLpTXSVQLVSnn1XGrKbHd2lS2dKbB9m4PiChLurRxa6Fc0HN7r0KDeJM6qIa3G8T4PoKap8fo2g3JmczNsVs0jIqLCmwOalFUEqmH9E5MD9ErJXkLnJpzcpDYAHO3sZjFIQq4pd9VTpZcpLrzTk3Jn09EE7Ws9i-j_CVgELNd1dbV4k2LgSF3Ku4DFm4hbSl8CFoo1MxUBy5a52EQtpnD69jM3WeN5cQhc7qMY5H4YDr-P-jeZp4bvAAAA__8jSHoW) was born in Uvalde. The last Texas governor to serve a two-year term and the first to serve a four-year term, Briscoe occupied the office from 1973 to 1979. A federal appeals court [upheld](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-OszAQBPCngQ5kLw6YwsXX0OVrr0T-sxtWMpAzeyF5-1PyCtf_Zkaar-t89Zx5u_33Kzp50j5veB4ZRbDMCx-yF443JuGI9z1zfPkVtxkU9I0yDXRz8pxfs6mTCyqNukanh6EfDWhr68XFpFRAQ10YTB9tDBaUHijaix17Dalm9y5TBjqtVaegJZssWR0A0hBHGiujMLHspZUFBZ_-oPzaH1jauK91dovI_ai6fxVMFUzneb5dxE2wHN8_vuDbVTB9ohVMvgjHjHPQSEQmNrFH3Rjypgl4CY2NQRHofhwptIusuS4u3U_OmY9-qIy6rZ7zZ1z-dtnDwW8AAAD__xi8gHM) a new Texas law that requires public schools to display the Ten Commandments in every classroom. Hit reply to let us know what you think of the ruling. The cost of living varies wildly across the United States, and for retirees, choosing the wrong state can cost up to six figures a year. On today's episode of _The Flyover Podcast_ , host Ayla Brown shares new data that ranks the cost of living in every U.S. state, and what groceries, housing, and healthcare actually cost depending on where you live. Plus, which states give retirees the most financial breathing room? Where does your state land, and what does it mean for your retirement? [Tune in here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykzzGO6yAQgOHT2F0sGGMDBcVr0uW1WyIMQzwSNhYeJfHtV7tX2AP8v_R9PfwjUKH9-T9s6PiTq9_xfRZkxuZXOrk2ik_KTBGPWiheYcPdg4D5JtQNRp8ClcurPrlFJCt7dFLr2SqQxvSrW6yIaJW2OEuIU9Jy0vM8mWlU4wTa9uR-ZkLBKKUYBQzZJJONXACSjjbbTglMxLUNvCLjJ5y5XPWFbYh164tbmY-zG_91cO_g3nBpYU9DuTq4HzXhh1uQfXPpeFMpdM66U-K5BSq_Pf9N_XLwHQAA__9xt2sj)! Later today, watch for a note from our partner, [Incogni](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMvK5CAUhJ_G7Dro0THJwsVAk13Pdpbi5ZgcMLEx9u3thzTzBv_yK4rio_7e7M1Rpn354zY07Z2K3fF1ZGwNq13paKVSWCg1CngvmcLHbbhb4KAvXF1A2ugof6zqovE8TqJDI4ZBTwrEOHar8UF6HZQKzv3yAqYJPEDyUgotkkLVkTnHuAIpBJcc-jTGMY3CA8QhTGliimOkVmrfVmz4dkfKn_LE2oeyddmsrd0PJn8zmBnMEV3uaQ9l2amnwmB2KdnA5FxSwmopMnnVDPQZf0HI4eSjPGpAJq8RIwXXMNpTioF-1Py_2VUT7y_KmQ49MMWXzVH-arSfnfc08C8AAP__4BeBrQ), on how to get your personal data off the internet, plus a special discount code for our readers. Property taxes in Texas are no small expense, and most homeowners don’t realize they can challenge what they owe. The key is timing. The deadline to protest your property tax assessment is May 15. That’s where today’s partner, [Resolute Property Tax Services](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMuOhCAQRb8Gd22gYHwsWHTScdeznaVBKLUSEAPYj7-fOOk_mO2pOjc39-c-3g152pZvE1CX1xzHDZ_ZYymYxpVyiYnsQnMhi3v0ZN8m4DYCh-bC1QXk6Az596gqpyfuelGhFm3b9ApE11WrntVk3CR4J8C0VoEyX2gbIZVte9MqUZE-w7gCKQSXHOq5c93ciQnAtbafe6Y4Oiox1WXFgi-TZ_-OD0y1jaHyei1lz0xeGQwMhkzLdux1whz9UXAv-XxjMDgGw0eEnsnhKGHM8UgWmbx9DgyaEwd0dAQmbxgM-Q-0JuyGlo3J23VP5EHeDW1V0m5_kveUm5YpvpzGX7HyvzkfGn4DAAD__2yQi-k), comes in to help you take action before it’s too late.

## Dells Giving $750 Million for UT Medical Center
Michael and Susan Dell announced a $750 million donation Tuesday to establish an "AI-native" hospital at UT Austin, becoming the first donors to [surpass](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOnDAQhJ8GOp9sww-mcJGG7tKmRIu9hk2MjezNcff2EXmFvxtpNKOZ79dzeQJFSttPONDyO-Ql4VUjMmNZdqqcC7mNApPDM0dyHzgwLVrqQche6G7xQPGz9K23q_STatGqcRymXitj2t1-dZNHF5xaDazKdMOowuq-AIJyZgDdkr3LZK87pWQn9SMYb4JRq9Z-dFOYml6iJ87lwTsyvqGG-MkvLA-XjzbanfmsTfej0XOj5-u6HiG_7xe33-iZ0e2Nng9yO2AU9W-FJDzGW5YTahVKrBQj5SR8TsCUUxUruD-UNgEkfKEXJrHnehJDFGfJv9FxW6w_rztYh7Hp5XYAxf-b-HskX1b_CwAA__9USoym) $1 billion in giving to the school. Michael Dell, the founder and CEO of Round Rock-based Dell Technologies, dropped out of UT Austin in the 1980s and has [contributed significantly](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL-OtSAQR58GO25wRMCC4mvs7tduaVDgOgmCwbl_fPuNu4-w3cmc5GTy-7pPd4cJ8-O_24KlTyxTDu8jBaJQpxUPKhWXB0bCJewl4XK6LeQJBCguJIdu8g7TOcnG21n4oW2CbbVWg4TWmGa1vYmd7xejFKi5871WUoPSRkqjBMyiQXvFhISubUUn4BaNN9G0M4DXyxAHJkXwSKXeaA0UPu6I6SyvUG9L2ZpkV6L9YN0_BiODMZZKzxwux2C80gxGIS8GBuOGy-pC4j6kxJ_E3fMgzFz3gm-YEpbMfcmOLph_Dw5r4PuKyWVaa9lPBmNTrd_flz-UZlI8Nofp5x_624ovC98BAAD___NXhfk) to the university over the last two decades. The historic gift will be used to [establish](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2unTAQBOCnMZ2RvXD5KVykobtpUyJjrw8r-Qfh5XLO20fkFdKNNNKn0fz5Xr8tRcqv3zah4Xcoa8a7RmTGc92pcjnJvSgwOTxKJPexCfMKCgapegnd6i3Fz9o33mzKz7pBo8dxmHvQ09TsBpTquml2ECDgqDY3wxZmP8Lw9eWshobMg6keOq1Vp6ANk5_CpDcAP7o5zKJX6InL2fKOjG9bQ_yUHzxbV1ITzc58VNH9ErAIWO77bl3e3FMKWB5bwKL6J2sBSyK3W4yyXtVm6TFGebG0V2XK0pdsmUqWCT05G6Wz6bhqu3OKzWn8cVOMVIdR9OqVLMV_E_j_jvsx8DcAAP__O4WCVQ) the UT Dell Medical Center and the UT Dell Campus for Advanced Research, which will employ advanced computing and AI in disease prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. The donation jumpstarts a university [initiative](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2upSAURp8GOwxsPYoFxTR2d9opDcJWd4JgZHt-3n7ieYXbfcXKysr372f6cRQprX_djpbfS54SvkpEZjynjQrnk_xKC5PHI0fyH7djmkBBJ1UroZmCo_iZ2irYWYVBV2h133dDC9qYarOz6Xww-HjMoPUDBhWcDzCgamZtXK8rsrdMtdBorRoF9WKCWYyeAULvh2UQrcJAnM-aN2R8u7LET37iWfu8V9FuzEcRzR8Bo4Dxzq-vL1ZjuASMt17AqNp7awHjTn5zGKVLQZaruCQDxijLdR6uFKnlTDFSTpKSXOlJaZWc5cXSXYUpCRir04bjdVOl60Wr1t1R_Obw7058WvgfAAD__3mchjA) to raise $10 billion for the medical center in the next 10 years, with the aim of ranking among the nation's top 10 hospitals during that time.
## Ten Commandments Law Upheld
A federal appeals court [overturned](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpTAMRb8mdHlKDCJQUGxDN9tuiUziPCwFgogZhr9f8X5hKru4ukfn_vuavpATb--_uNIgPzFPG10lkQgd08JF8sH-zVHY054T-xtX2iYw0GrTaKingJzuqanCMJvQ24oG61zbN2C7rlqGOLvookUDHmsb2xoctm0_227uA3lX8fCUmQZqa01t4BW70MXOzgDB-T72qjEUWPLxkoWEfrDEdOdvOl4-r1UaFpG9qPqPglHBeF3X6ywoOeD9BBSMj8StYHzMnoPCeVMwPlwFo2me3yoYIwU6MGncd8JUtM_nIfo4ExX9Aev9nBN7XfySc9IeNx247AlvLbRpa7TP64pbWGmTh9X1DpreGmOcgrE6hrBfnBKX1qnGvFfk9LGQ323_PcD_AAAA__-2wZit) an injunction blocking several school districts from implementing a new state law mandating the display of the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms. In a 9-8 ruling, the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals [rejected](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOpCAURp9GOwwgO2JBsY3dbLulQbjoTUBcuI4zb79xXuGvv-TkfOfvc35ajLivf2wCQ--Q5x2uGoEIyrxhpVzQrRgIHRw5ovvYBPssuXwwrpjsZ28xfmbVerNwP4oWjBiGx6ik0LrdzC-ng-fKikX1DgQHq5SAAFyphetet2huGFeyF4L3XHZBex20WKT0gxvD2CgOHimXjjYgeNsa4ie_oHQupzaajeioTf-7kVMjp-u6upDf94t7b-R0axO62sgpgIdiI3P5LMTOY8vRV_ZlsmgvVuDfiQX3lRHszOWU7O4T7FTZcS4RHXPR1lpyTrUtxh8Xxoj1MTSKr8li_CrRz0K-jPwfAAD__6dqjK4) the argument that the law violates the First Amendment's establishment clause or the free exercise clause. "_To Plaintiffs, merely exposing children to religious language is enough to make the displays engines of coercive indoctrination. We disagree_ ," Judge Stuart Kyle Duncan [wrote](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OhCAYAJ8GOw18AkJBcbnEbq-8Kw3yo1-CYoRdd9_-cvsK10w5k8zPbbpZTLgvX3YLpj5jnvZwlRRqDee0Yqn5RLdgrOjCkRO6l93CPgEF2VLeQj95i-k18cabmXrNmmDYMEjNgSnVrMZywSjjSs0xwqD57KQQTA7OU6k0dQ2aPxnl0DNGewpdVF5FxWYAPzgdNeE0eKz57OoaanjaEtMrP8LZubw1yay1HoX0HwRGAuN1XZ2zorsXl-9nLd2SHwTGfOCOeS8ExuM-ExhBvNEKKrVoP79pd_jYnMYfF6aERQ6E02WzmN6Z-r85DwO_AQAA__8YtHbA) for the majority. The ACLU of Texas, which is representing the families who are challenging the statute, [vowed](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDvOpDAQhE9jMiPbIDCBg03IZtMNkR9taMkP1vYMM7f_xVzhT7urPlXVv8f20Bgw7X91BNXePm8JrhqgNSjbgbXlgnZH39DCmQPaj46QNsHERNlIxbA5jeGzjZ1ThrmFd6D4PE_LKLiU3aGkN0770UhpgQ9sAmbBTG5x3Bq7sKVDdcPYKAbO2cBE76WTXnIjhJvt4hcyMnDYcunbAQ3euvrwyS8ovc2xC-po7axk-EPESsR6XVdvTb1b3H8i1q-FiPU-EbF6cFB0oPo8QYdKbX6WRp_nkYOr9CumQV-0wP8nFkw7bZCozTHq5CKkVikmaoOuteQcb2RXlDsvDAHrNJOR7VFj-KZrv9v0pcRPAAAA__87bZCP) to appeal the ruling to the U.S. Supreme Court, saying the decision threatens the "separation of church and state, and the freedom of families to choose how, when, and if to provide their children with religious instruction." Attorney General Ken Paxton, whose office is defending the mandate, [called](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OpDAQhJ8GMpDd-PgJHFxCNpdeiDx2e2ipwcjuHYa3X7GvsFlJJVXp-_4_locjpv31z21o5RPTsuNZGEUwLysVSZn8i6KQxyMx-cttuC-goG-UaaBbgiO-FlMH-1Rh0jVaPQz9ZECPY71aDAZ6pUI3mCHoXk9G9S6a6CZvvAq6JnuPKQOd1qpT0MYxjHHUT4Aw-ClOlVEYSFJuZUXBjyuRr_TG3Pq01WxXkaNU3d8K5grm8zzby60p3W0F841TweyykGe8Y8SA2XHjjgMdl8anryyNY05naQT3BsD0-g8Y3a6ycZ1tOE5iptIPlVGvzRH_XMvvhL0tfAcAAP___82AIA) the decision a "major victory for Texas and our moral values."

On this day in 1923, future Democrat governor was born in Uvalde. A federal appeals court a new Texas law that requires public schools to display the Ten Commandments in every classroom. Hit reply to let us know what you think of the ruling. Plus, which states give retirees the most financial breathing room? Where does your state land, and what does it mean for your retirement? ! Michael Dell, the founder and CEO of Round Rock-based Dell Technologies, dropped out of UT Austin in the 1980s and has to the university over the last two decades. The historic gift will be used to the UT Dell Medical Center and the UT Dell Campus for Advanced Research, which will employ advanced computing and AI in disease prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. The donation jumpstarts a university to raise $10 billion for the medical center in the next 10 years, with the aim of ranking among the nation's top 10 hospitals during that time. Ten Commandments Law Upheld A federal appeals court an injunction blocking several school districts from implementing a new state law mandating the display of the Ten Commandments in public school classrooms. In a 9-8 ruling, the 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals the argument that the law violates the First Amendment's establishment clause or the free exercise clause. "To Plaintiffs, merely exposing children to religious language is enough to make the displays engines of coercive indoctrination. We disagree ," Judge Stuart Kyle Duncan for the majority. The ACLU of Texas, which is representing the families who are challenging the statute, to appeal the ruling to the U.S. Supreme Court, saying the decision threatens the "separation of church and state, and the freedom of families to choose how, when, and if to provide their children with religious instruction." Attorney General Ken Paxton, whose office is defending the mandate, the decision a "major victory for Texas and our moral values." Texas NFL Teams Set for Draft Night The NFL draft kicks off tonight, and both Texas NFL teams have first-round picks: the Dallas Cowboys at No. 12 and No. 20, and the Houston Texans at No. 28. Mock drafts consistently have them taking a defender with both first-round picks, but some pundits think they as high as No. 3 to get an elite pass rusher. The Texans, meanwhile, are expected to keep rebuilding an offensive line that buckled during C.J. Stroud's rocky 2025 campaign. name: The Maine Wire
subject: The SPLC Was Paying the Klan; Maine’s Press and Politicians Were Citing Them
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** The SPLC Was Paying the Klan; Maine’s Press and Politicians Were Citing Them (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=2ca75746db&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Steve Robinson on Apr 22, 2026 06:48 pm
For years, when a Maine politician wanted to lend borrowed credibility to a partisan smear, or when a Bangor Daily News reporter wanted an authoritative-sounding quote to confirm the premise of their story, they would cite the Southern Poverty Law Center. name: State Representative Ryan Chamberlin
subject: Property Tax Update From Rep. Chamberlin
body:
Property Tax Update From Rep. Chamberlin

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/repryanchamberlin/newsletter-from-rep-ryan-chamberlin-kqs1grv7ud-12933547?e=d1c1b9864f)
---
| | | | |
---
| |
# Property Tax Update From Rep. Chamberlin
---
| | Friends and Neighbors,

In 2023, after my 1st election, **I filed a bill proposing the elimination of Property Taxes.** Then in 2024, 2025, and 2026, I filed major relief bills and my **Freedom 1,2,3 plan** to TOTALLY eliminate and replace the current property tax system.
The strategy laid out will **COMPLETELY** fund our Police, Fire, and Schools all while allowing **FLORIDA** to become the first state in the United States where you can actually own your property.
Now, after fighting for Florida citizens property tax relief, on April 1st, I released a plan that will ensure our success. _(Click the link below watch the full 10 minute press conference (and Follow me on Facebook for ongoing updates)_
---
[|
---](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=bf64e16dd0&e=d1c1b9864f)
| | Since the Press Conference, a series of articles and interviews are starting to pop.
Here is an article by The Florida Roundup titled:**Why Rep. Ryan Chamberlin wants to eliminate and replace Florida's property taxes**
_A Republican state representative wants to shake up Florida's entire property tax system by eliminating and replacing it._
_The effort to cut or eliminate property taxes in the state has been a top priority for Gov. Ron DeSantis, but so far, there's no official plan._
_Supporters believe cutting these taxes will help with affordability. WUSF's Douglas Soule previously reported how_[ _**critics warn**_](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=53b599248d&e=d1c1b9864f) _it would affect local services and require fees to be raised elsewhere._
_Lawmakers have wanted to put a measure on the November ballot to reduce property taxes. But during the session, the Florida House passed a sweeping plan that the Senate did not support._
[_**The session ended without a budget,**_](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=ae4e8a938c&e=d1c1b9864f)_so lawmakers will have to come back to hash it out. The budget has to be approved by July 1 to avoid a partial government shutdown._
_Since the likelihood that the two chambers will pass a constitutional amendment this year is on shaky ground, state Rep. Ryan Chamberlin (R-Marion County) has a backup plan._
_He wants to launch a campaign to collect enough signatures on a petition that would eliminate all property taxes on all properties in Florida. If he gets enough signatures, it could be on the ballot in 2028._
_On "The Florida Roundup," Chamberlin said he'd have a replacement plan following elimination, why he doesn't support the current system, and more._
_I think we should come to a place in this state — be the first state in the United States where people can actually own their property rather than rent it from the government._**Do you support doing away with all property taxes in Florida?**_Absolutely. I support doing away with the system of property taxes that also involves a replacement plan, which I have unveiled this past year. At least a first draft of one.__I do believe that we can eliminate and replace. I'm a believer that the government should not have a lien on anybody's property._**Would you include eliminating property taxes for school districts?**_I would propose eliminating — eventually — all property taxes.__Initially, homestead is where it seems like we're going to start.__But again, if I believe that the government having a lien on a personal home property is wrong, I also don't like the fact that we would have liens in school districts or liens on properties in any district.__"We don't need to cut. We just need to figure out a different way to generate that revenue."__I think with a $1.8 trillion economy, we can figure out a better way to generate the revenue to absolutely not cut any funding when it comes to fire departments, our safety, our schools.__We don't need to cut. We just need to figure out a different way to generate that revenue._**Eliminate the property taxes — all property taxes on all property: Residential vacation homes, commercial condominiums, school taxes, public safety taxes, all taxes, that's eventually what you'd like to get to. Is that accurate?**_Absolutely.__I think we should come to a place in this state — be the first state in the United States where people can actually own their property rather than rent it from the government.__I don't like the current system. I think it was designed in a time when our state was a different size, a different place. I wouldn't have agreed with it back then either, but it's gotten out of control._**How would you propose local governments and school districts, police and fire pay for existing services and personnel?**_I think there are several ways we can do it. I have presented a few ways based on transactions. One, we would put a one-time sales fee on property.__Rather than taxing people $3,000 to $5,000 a year for the rest of their lives on one purchase, we could figure out a reasonable fee where they buy their property to put some money into the system.__I know we have 140-plus million travelers in and out of the state of Florida. They're not helping cover any of the safety fees that are on the backs of the homeowners paying for the whole state right now.__Yet, when they come here, if they're in trouble, our police take care of them, our fire takes care of them. And there's nothing that's being contributed to relieve the burden of property taxes when it comes to that.__[If] someone says, "Ryan, are you open to raising the sales tax at all?" If I have to make a choice between property tax and raising the sales tax one cent or two cents.__Every one cent in the state of Florida generates billions of dollars. We can get to the number fairly rapidly if we look at these creative transactional ways.__"I don't like the current system. I think it was designed in a time when our state was a different size, a different place."__One reason why I would lean toward a sales tax increase if we had to — I don't know that we have to.__But if we had to is because again, the 140 million people that travel to our state, then they're spending so much of that money there, it's actually relieving if we move it off the backs of the homeowners.__It would be one of the biggest tax cuts for the citizens of Florida in history. So I think we look at the numbers and figure it out._
---
|
---
| | You can read and listen to the full interview by [_**clicking this link.**_](https://ryanchamberlin.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ec15c6093052c02efbd22b6cd&id=410968ce3e&e=d1c1b9864f) This story was compiled from interviews conducted by Tom Hudson for "The Florida Roundup."

clean_body: Property Tax Update From Rep. Chamberlin | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Property Tax Update From Rep. Chamberlin | | Friends and Neighbors, In 2023, after my 1st election, I filed a bill proposing the elimination of Property Taxes. Then in 2024, 2025, and 2026, I filed major relief bills and my Freedom 1,2,3 plan to TOTALLY eliminate and replace the current property tax system. The strategy laid out will COMPLETELY fund our Police, Fire, and Schools all while allowing FLORIDA to become the first state in the United States where you can actually own your property. Now, after fighting for Florida citizens property tax relief, on April 1st, I released a plan that will ensure our success. (Click the link below watch the full 10 minute press conference (and Follow me on Facebook for ongoing updates) | | Since the Press Conference, a series of articles and interviews are starting to pop. Here is an article by The Florida Roundup titled:Why Rep. Ryan Chamberlin wants to eliminate and replace Florida's property taxes A Republican state representative wants to shake up Florida's entire property tax system by eliminating and replacing it. The effort to cut or eliminate property taxes in the state has been a top priority for Gov. Ron DeSantis, but so far, there's no official plan. Supporters believe cutting these taxes will help with affordability. WUSF's Douglas Soule previously reported how it would affect local services and require fees to be raised elsewhere. Lawmakers have wanted to put a measure on the November ballot to reduce property taxes. But during the session, the Florida House passed a sweeping plan that the Senate did not support. lawmakers will have to come back to hash it out. The budget has to be approved by July 1 to avoid a partial government shutdown. Since the likelihood that the two chambers will pass a constitutional amendment this year is on shaky ground, state Rep. Ryan Chamberlin (R-Marion County) has a backup plan. He wants to launch a campaign to collect enough signatures on a petition that would eliminate all property taxes on all properties in Florida. If he gets enough signatures, it could be on the ballot in 2028. On "The Florida Roundup," Chamberlin said he'd have a replacement plan following elimination, why he doesn't support the current system, and more. I think we should come to a place in this state — be the first state in the United States where people can actually own their property rather than rent it from the government.Do you support doing away with all property taxes in Florida?Absolutely. I support doing away with the system of property taxes that also involves a replacement plan, which I have unveiled this past year. At least a first draft of one.I do believe that we can eliminate and replace. I'm a believer that the government should not have a lien on anybody's property.Would you include eliminating property taxes for school districts?I would propose eliminating — eventually — all property taxes.Initially, homestead is where it seems like we're going to start.But again, if I believe that the government having a lien on a personal home property is wrong, I also don't like the fact that we would have liens in school districts or liens on properties in any district."We don't need to cut. We just need to figure out a different way to generate that revenue."I think with a $1.8 trillion economy, we can figure out a better way to generate the revenue to absolutely not cut any funding when it comes to fire departments, our safety, our schools.We don't need to cut. We just need to figure out a different way to generate that revenue.Eliminate the property taxes — all property taxes on all property: Residential vacation homes, commercial condominiums, school taxes, public safety taxes, all taxes, that's eventually what you'd like to get to. Is that accurate?Absolutely.I think we should come to a place in this state — be the first state in the United States where people can actually own their property rather than rent it from the government.I don't like the current system. I think it was designed in a time when our state was a different size, a different place. I wouldn't have agreed with it back then either, but it's gotten out of control.How would you propose local governments and school districts, police and fire pay for existing services and personnel?I think there are several ways we can do it. I have presented a few ways based on transactions. One, we would put a one-time sales fee on property.Rather than taxing people $3,000 to $5,000 a year for the rest of their lives on one purchase, we could figure out a reasonable fee where they buy their property to put some money into the system.I know we have 140-plus million travelers in and out of the state of Florida. They're not helping cover any of the safety fees that are on the backs of the homeowners paying for the whole state right now.Yet, when they come here, if they're in trouble, our police take care of them, our fire takes care of them. And there's nothing that's being contributed to relieve the burden of property taxes when it comes to that.[If] someone says, "Ryan, are you open to raising the sales tax at all?" If I have to make a choice between property tax and raising the sales tax one cent or two cents.Every one cent in the state of Florida generates billions of dollars. We can get to the number fairly rapidly if we look at these creative transactional ways."I don't like the current system. I think it was designed in a time when our state was a different size, a different place."One reason why I would lean toward a sales tax increase if we had to — I don't know that we have to.But if we had to is because again, the 140 million people that travel to our state, then they're spending so much of that money there, it's actually relieving if we move it off the backs of the homeowners.It would be one of the biggest tax cuts for the citizens of Florida in history. So I think we look at the numbers and figure it out. | | | You can read and listen to the full interview by This story was compiled from interviews conducted by Tom Hudson for "The Florida Roundup." | | HERE IS ALSO SOME ADDITIONAL COVERAGE ON THIS ISSUE: | | | STAY CONNECTED FOR UPDATES: For continued updates and future announcements on property tax reform, I encourage you to follow along on Facebook: Staying informed is important as this conversation continues, and your input will help shape what comes next. As always, please feel free to reach out and share your thoughts. | | Best regards, | | | | | | | | | | | | | Ryan Chamberlin**State RepresentativeDistrict 24 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2026 Ryan Chamberlin for State Representative. All rights reserved._

name: Michelle Witthoeft, Stand in the Gap
subject: Get Justice for Ashli Babbitt
body:
Ever since my daughter, Ashli Babbitt, was shot by a Capitol Police officer, the Deep State and the fake news media have done nothing but spread lies, slander, and smears about her.

| | |
---
| | [](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkD1uhTAQhE9jOhCsbX4KF5Ge6F7alGgxa3BiDLKX5PoRT8oB0s58Gn2aj-f0RB98XN9xJ5PYTSlPK_HnldlbckfCvAU_4zx75glqaMtalSBvDorFLHbBxhZkmq5rB6U7JYvNzI0F3QB1qh9m1dfktGs7lN3Qa5SuL7y5p2oFsoEaAKpWat3rvnXaDsNMjVA1BkpcJTqvOXiLkTF_uSNZquyxF8FszGcW8k3AKGDMZK9EFUZaDr6JO2OMS-ljyRuVK54CxhNLtOyPKOR48T5Z3E_0axTy8WckoH01R2SKLOQjsSuSWc4fH4LPbSdUve7ow8uD___at4HfAAAA__9YIHrw) Friend,

Ever since my daughter, Ashli Babbitt, was shot by a Capitol Police officer, the Deep State and the fake news media have done nothing but spread lies, slander, and smears about her.

**I’m writing to you today because I want you to know who my daughter really was, how her rights were trampled over, and how you can help[get justice for Ashli and all the victims of the January 6th political persecution](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkD1uhTAQhE9jOhCsbX4KF5Ge6F7alGgxa3BiDLKX5PoRT8oB0s58Gn2aj-f0RB98XN9xJ5PYTSlPK_HnldlbckfCvAU_4zx75glqaMtalSBvDorFLHbBxhZkmq5rB6U7JYvNzI0F3QB1qh9m1dfktGs7lN3Qa5SuL7y5p2oFsoEaAKpWat3rvnXaDsNMjVA1BkpcJTqvOXiLkTF_uSNZquyxF8FszGcW8k3AKGDMZK9EFUZaDr6JO2OMS-ljyRuVK54CxhNLtOyPKOR48T5Z3E_0axTy8WckoH01R2SKLOQjsSuSWc4fH4LPbSdUve7ow8uD___at4HfAAAA__9YIHrw).**

Ashli was a proud American patriot and veteran who loved God and her family and was passionate about defending our Constitution.

That’s why, on January 6, 2021, Ashli went to Washington to exercise her constitutional rights in protest of the certification of the 2020 election results.

**But on that day, a Capitol Hill Police officer shot Ashli in cold blood in a crowded hallway inside the Capitol.**

People have told me it’s time to move on, that the forces I am up against are just too big to fight, and that I will never get justice for Ashli.

BUT I WILL NEVER STOP FIGHTING! Not just for Ashli but _for _everyone_ who had their rights trampled that day_.

**Many turned their back on those involved with the January 6th protest. However, there is one very special organization that wasn’t afraid to fight back. In fact, they are still fighting for justice for January 6th victims and former defendants.**

**That organization is Stand in the Gap.**

[Now, I want to return the favor. Will you support Stand in the Gap’s extraordinary mission today?](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkD1uhTAQhE9jOhCsbX4KF5Ge6F7alGgxa3BiDLKX5PoRT8oB0s58Gn2aj-f0RB98XN9xJ5PYTSlPK_HnldlbckfCvAU_4zx75glqaMtalSBvDorFLHbBxhZkmq5rB6U7JYvNzI0F3QB1qh9m1dfktGs7lN3Qa5SuL7y5p2oFsoEaAKpWat3rvnXaDsNMjVA1BkpcJTqvOXiLkTF_uSNZquyxF8FszGcW8k3AKGDMZK9EFUZaDr6JO2OMS-ljyRuVK54CxhNLtOyPKOR48T5Z3E_0axTy8WckoH01R2SKLOQjsSuSWc4fH4LPbSdUve7ow8uD___at4HfAAAA__9YIHrw)
[Donate](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkD1uhTAQhE9jOhCsbX4KF5Ge6F7alGgxa3BiDLKX5PoRT8oB0s58Gn2aj-f0RB98XN9xJ5PYTSlPK_HnldlbckfCvAU_4zx75glqaMtalSBvDorFLHbBxhZkmq5rB6U7JYvNzI0F3QB1qh9m1dfktGs7lN3Qa5SuL7y5p2oFsoEaAKpWat3rvnXaDsNMjVA1BkpcJTqvOXiLkTF_uSNZquyxF8FszGcW8k3AKGDMZK9EFUZaDr6JO2OMS-ljyRuVK54CxhNLtOyPKOR48T5Z3E_0axTy8WckoH01R2SKLOQjsSuSWc4fH4LPbSdUve7ow8uD___at4HfAAAA__9YIHrw)
I’m so thankful for President Trump’s pardons, but so many lives have been ruined...
* Former January 6th defendants lost years of their lives while they rotted in jail.
* Their families lost homes as they paid crushing legal expenses.
* Some January 6th defendants lost all hope and ended their lives, leaving their families with nothing but a memory.
* And my daughter Ashli paid the ultimate sacrifice.

There’s nothing that can be done for my Ashli, but there is work to be done to make things right for other J6ers.

**Stand in the Gap was founded to _“stand in the gap”_ for the January 6th defendants and their families.**

Now that President Trump has issued pardons for these victims of political persecution, **Stand in the Gap is on a mission to help them navigate the challenging process of re-entry into society after being incarcerated.**

These men and women need help and healing — physically, mentally, emotionally, and financially.

We must let them know that they are not forgotten.

This begins by helping to ensure they have the resources they need to rebuild their lives and reclaim their dignity.

[By donating today, you can help Stand in the Gap provide help, hope, and healing for these American patriots. Click below to make a tax-deductible donation today! >>](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyUkD1uhTAQhE9jOhCsbX4KF5Ge6F7alGgxa3BiDLKX5PoRT8oB0s58Gn2aj-f0RB98XN9xJ5PYTSlPK_HnldlbckfCvAU_4zx75glqaMtalSBvDorFLHbBxhZkmq5rB6U7JYvNzI0F3QB1qh9m1dfktGs7lN3Qa5SuL7y5p2oFsoEaAKpWat3rvnXaDsNMjVA1BkpcJTqvOXiLkTF_uSNZquyxF8FszGcW8k3AKGDMZK9EFUZaDr6JO2OMS-ljyRuVK54CxhNLtOyPKOR48T5Z3E_0axTy8WckoH01R2SKLOQjsSuSWc4fH4LPbSdUve7ow8uD___at4HfAAAA__9YIHrw)

clean_body: Ever since my daughter, Ashli Babbitt, was shot by a Capitol Police officer, the Deep State and the fake news media have done nothing but spread lies, slander, and smears about her. | | | | | Friend, Ever since my daughter, Ashli Babbitt, was shot by a Capitol Police officer, the Deep State and the fake news media have done nothing but spread lies, slander, and smears about her. I’m writing to you today because I want you to know who my daughter really was, how her rights were trampled over, and how you can help. Ashli was a proud American patriot and veteran who loved God and her family and was passionate about defending our Constitution. That’s why, on January 6, 2021, Ashli went to Washington to exercise her constitutional rights in protest of the certification of the 2020 election results. But on that day, a Capitol Hill Police officer shot Ashli in cold blood in a crowded hallway inside the Capitol. People have told me it’s time to move on, that the forces I am up against are just too big to fight, and that I will never get justice for Ashli. BUT I WILL NEVER STOP FIGHTING! Not just for Ashli but for everyone who had their rights trampled that day. Many turned their back on those involved with the January 6th protest. However, there is one very special organization that wasn’t afraid to fight back. In fact, they are still fighting for justice for January 6th victims and former defendants. That organization is Stand in the Gap. I’m so thankful for President Trump’s pardons, but so many lives have been ruined... Former January 6th defendants lost years of their lives while they rotted in jail. Their families lost homes as they paid crushing legal expenses. Some January 6th defendants lost all hope and ended their lives, leaving their families with nothing but a memory. And my daughter Ashli paid the ultimate sacrifice. There’s nothing that can be done for my Ashli, but there is work to be done to make things right for other J6ers. Stand in the Gap was founded to “stand in the gap” for the January 6th defendants and their families. Now that President Trump has issued pardons for these victims of political persecution, Stand in the Gap is on a mission to help them navigate the challenging process of re-entry into society after being incarcerated. These men and women need help and healing — physically, mentally, emotionally, and financially. We must let them know that they are not forgotten. This begins by helping to ensure they have the resources they need to rebuild their lives and reclaim their dignity. As I write this, some J6ers are still battling for their freedom as judges around the country try to find ways around the President’s pardons. That’s why the work of Stand in the Gap is so essential right now. Stand in the Gap is helping J6ers rebuild their shattered lives. They’re providing housing, work opportunities, and healing to those who have been granted their freedom. They’ve just scratched the surface of what they can do, but they need more resources to make every one of these American patriots whole. I won’t stop fighting for justice for Ashli until I take my dying breath. To honor her memory, I’m also fighting for the men and women who share her commitment and passion for our great country. That’s why I choose to support Stand in the Gap — and I hope you will, too. Thank you in advance for any support you can generously provide. Sincerely, Michelle "Micki" Witthoeft P.S. My daughter, Ashli Babbitt, was murdered by the government on January 6th. Her rights were trampled that day, but we can still fight for justice for her and the 1,500 J6ers who were hunted down and treated like enemies of the state. These people weren’t sent to prison for what they did. They were sentenced for what they believed. That’s not supposed to happen in America! But it did. Whether you donate $35 or $2,500 to help Stand in the Gap support these political prisoners, I hope you do so today. Thank you for your generosity!
